caption: This educational presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" focuses on "Tutorial – 3" and discusses the "Figure of Merit (FOM)". The slide features a thermodynamic diagram plotting temperature against entropy (S), showing a cycle with processes labeled "T=const", "p=const", and "D=const". Two calculations are displayed on the graph: "$\frac{W}{\dot{m}_r} = 2772.1$" and "$\frac{W}{\dot{m}_r} = 476.0$". At the bottom, the text "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" is visible alongside an NPTEL logo. As the sequence progresses, a formula for FOM is introduced: "$\text{FOM} = \frac{\frac{W}{\dot{m}_r}}{\frac{W}{\dot{m}_r}}$". In the final frames, the specific values from the graph are substituted into this formula, resulting in the final calculation: "$\frac{476.0}{2772.1} = 0.1717$". The layout remains static while the mathematical derivation is incrementally written on the screen.
